name: prog
description: Builder and programmer tooling. The same world is the IDE: tools inspect and mutate the live object graph, with no design-time/runtime split. The catalog deliberately preserves LambdaCore's builder/programmer authority split by installing them as player classes. Builder class membership exposes object/data tools. Programmer class membership exposes source/metadata tools, while progbit and wizbit remain the hard authority facts.

classes.description: Builder player class. Actors inheriting from `$builder` expose object creation, reparenting, recycling, data-property setting, and structure-oriented inspection/search as their own MCP tools. Builder class membership is the authoring surface, not programmer authority: these verbs cannot install code, change executable behavior, or adjust source/perms metadata. Engine builtins check the invoking actor and object policy, not the wrapper verb owner.

verbs.name: look
inspect
search
create
chparent
recycle
recycle_command
set_property

classes.description: Programmer player class. Actors inheriting from `$programmer` expose source-aware inspection, verb resolution, source listing, source search, verb install with dry-run diagnostics, metadata-only verb edits, property-definition metadata, and traces as their own MCP tools. Programmer class membership exposes the interface; the programmer or wizard flag remains the hard authority check. The surface is source-level; bytecode and VM internals are deliberately absent.

verbs.name: look
inspect
resolve_verb
list_verb
search
install_verb
set_verb_info
set_property_info
edit_verb
trace
eval
force_recycle

classes.description: Room-like editor base class. It owns per-actor edit sessions and source-buffer lifecycle verbs. Presence, focus, wait, and any conversational behavior come from ordinary room/actor mechanics; the editor adds only view, edit, dry-run, save, pause, abort, and session introspection.

verbs.name: look
what
view
replace
insert
delete
dry_run
save
pause
abort

classes.description: Verb-source editor room. Sessions point at a target object and ordered verb descriptor, keep a source buffer with expected_version, and save through the same source-level programmer install path used by MCP tools.

seed_hooks.name: Verb Editor
seed_hooks.description: A room-like source editor for verb bodies. Actors enter through programmer edit_verb, edit a per-actor buffer, dry-run diagnostics, and save back to the target object without moving the target into the room.
